'''Sunrider's Destiny''' was a unique aquamarine [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]].<ref name="SWG:SD" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was given to a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individual by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sarguillo]] in the village of [[Aurilia]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]].<ref name="SWG:CEL" /> When this crystal was properly tuned to be used in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], only the person who tuned it could use that lightsaber. When used in a lightsaber, the crystal caused the blade generated to be [[Color/Legends|teal]] in color.<ref name="SWG:SD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sunrider's Destiny appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:SD" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> It shared the name of the [[Sunrider family]], which included the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Nomi Sunrider]] and her daughter [[Vima Sunrider]].<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

Sunrider's Destiny was added to the game with the introduction of the village of Aurilia and the "Jedi Trials" system,<ref name="SWG:CEL" /> released with the [[W:c:swg:Publish 10 Notes for 31 August 04|Publish 10]] on [[August 31]], [[2004]].<ref name="SWG:P10">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=63330|text=Publish 10 The Jedi Trials}}</ref> The crystal was made unavailable following the release of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]], a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]], that totally revamped the Jedi profession and removed the Aurilia questline.<ref name="PA2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> Finally, Sunrider's Destiny was reintroduced in the game through the "[[W:c:swg:Rare Loot System|Rare Loot System]]," released with the [[W:c:swg:Hotfix 17.10|Hotfix 17.10]] on [[October 7]], [[2010]].<ref name="H17.10">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68952&month=102010|text=Star Wars Galaxies: Hotfix 17.10 -- Thursday, October 7, 2010}}</ref>
